title: Authorization, Tenancy, Object, Function, and Property Policy · Compiler Case: Object Level Authorization
summary: Routes check a broad role but not object ownership, tenant filters are optional, update bodies bind protected fields, and denials leak which records exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts object level authorization increment in deny-by-default resource authorization decision. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Load only enough identity or policy data to enforce ownership and object access without trusting a user-selected identifier.
Authorize a validated subject, resource, action, context, and requested properties at the resource boundary before effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Object Level Authorization · Theory Object Level Authorization
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6635 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce tenant, object action, and property policy as one deny-by-default decision; reject a replay before any external effect; then emit a server error and retain one low-cardinality causal trace. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Object Level Authorization contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Node and Express server Predict Object Level Authorization · Theory Object Level Authorization.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for load only enough identity or policy data to enforce ownership and object access without trusting a user-selected identifier. For Node and Express server Predict Object Level Authorization · Theory Object Level Authorization, keep Object Level Authorization connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name runtime, method, authority, route, unknown-input boundary, identity, resource budgets, middleware and dependency ownership, response effect, cleanup, and recovery; run a pure deterministic TypeScript decision model; then change one input or failure and state the Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, or production transfer gate. Apply that move specifically to Object Level Authorization while producing deny-by-default resource authorization decision.
